title: triticum turgidum subsp. durum
intro: Triticum turgidum subsp. durum, also known as durum wheat or emmer wheat, is an ancient species of wheat that is primarily used for making semolina flour. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as arthritis, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of durum wheat include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting healthy digestion. Herbal preparations of durum wheat, such as semolina powder or tea, can be made by drying and grinding the grains, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
